reasoning_model_1: Model 1 accurately follows the text instructions, delivering the line 'No, don't come closer! I am so scared.' Although it expands 'I'm' to 'I am', this does not negatively impact the semantic integrity. The emotional performance is strong and aligns well with the 'fearful' prompt. Specifically, at 00:00, the word 'No' is delivered with a breathless, high-pitched trembling quality. At 00:01-00:02, 'don't come closer' features a pleading, unstable pitch characteristic of fear. At 00:03, 'I am so scared' is spoken with a consistent shaky vocal fry and urgency. The pacing allows for the dramatic tension required by the emotion.
reasoning_model_2: Model 2 fails significantly in both content accuracy and emotional delivery. First, regarding content, the model hallucinates a large amount of unrequested text starting at 00:03 ('My voice trembles, my breath comes short...'), turning a dialogue prompt into a narration. Second, regarding the emotion, the delivery of the target sentence (00:00-00:02) is rushed, flat, and monotonous. It sounds like a narrator quickly reading a script rather than a character experiencing fear. There is no trembling, pitch variation, or intensity associated with the requested emotion.
model_comparison: Model 1 is the clear winner. It correctly isolates the target text and performs it with the requested paralinguistic feature (fear), utilizing vocal trembling, breathlessness, and appropriate pacing. Model 2 fails on multiple levels: it hallucinates extensive text not found in the prompt and delivers the actual target text with a flat, unemotional, and rushed narrative tone. According to the scoring standards, Model 2 receives a 0 due to the major content addition (hallucination) and the complete absence of the required emotional affect.
